rulings.Case Title: SHU CHUN WANG VS. MERITOUCH, A GENERAL PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Debtor Sheng Li Chinese Massage, Llcs Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 23, 2015, Line 1, CREDITOR SHU WANG'S Motion To Compel Debtor Sheng Li Chinese Massage, Llcs Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Mangini, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Creditor Shu Chun Wang's motion to compel debtor Sheng Li Chinese Massage, LLC ("LLC") to respond to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is GRANTED. LLC is to serve objection-free written responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One and produce documents by no later than October 9, 2015. Sanctions against LLC and LLC's counsel are imposed in the amount of $500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to manginilaw@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MURRAY JR. VS. ANOUSHIRAVAN MASSOUMI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 23, 2015, Line 15, PLAINTIFF THOMAS MURRAY'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment. The motion is granted. The court enters Plaintiff's form of judgment. Plaintiff served a revised form of judgment that incorporated Defendant's original objections. Defendant failed to object to that revised judgment within 10 days in compliance with CRC 3.1590(j). Defendant fails to demonstrate just cause for relief under subsection (m). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN F GREENWALD et al VS. STEVEN A MINISINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel Verifications To Written Discovery And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 23, 2015, Line 2, Defendant's Motion To Compel Verifications To Written Discovery And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Mangini, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Steven A. Minisini and Jennifer T. Chow's motion to compel verifications to written discovery from plaintiffs Stephen F. Greenwald and Rochelle D. Alpert is MOOT as a result of defendant's serving the requested verifications during the pendency of the immediate motion. Sanctions against plaintiff Greenwald his counsel are imposed in the amount of $450. Separate sanctions in the amount of $450 are imposed against plaintiff Alpert and her counsel. The fact that defendants belatedly cured their failures to verify discovery responses does not protect them from sanctions exposure as plaintiffs were forced to pursue the immediate motion to secure verifications. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to manginilaw@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL MCCREADY VS. BEAULIEU GROUP, LLC, A GEORGIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Defendants First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,420 Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 23, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS BEAULIEU GROUP, LLC, BEAULIEU COMMERCIAL, BEAULIEU OF AMERICA, BOLYU, INC., JO DUNHAM'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Defendants First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,420 Memorandum Of Points And Authorities. Pro Tem Judge David Mangini, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of defendants Beaulieu Group, LLC; Beaulieu Commercial; Beaulieu of America; Bolyu, Inc.; and Joann Dunham to compel plaintiff Cheryl McCready to further respond to defendants' Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is GRANTED. By no later than October 2, 2015, McCready is to: (1) serve amended written responses to Requests Nos. 2, 4, and 14 in full compliance the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure Section 2031.230 in the event of inability to comply with any aspect said Requests; and (2) produce all electronically stored information responsive to Requests Nos. 2, 4, and 14 in conformance with Code of Civil Procedure Section 2031.280(d)(1). Objections or claims of privilege not asserted in McCready's prior responses to the subject Requests may be included in the forthcoming amended written responses. Sanctions against McCready and McCready's counsel are imposed in the amount of $1,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to manginilaw@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

The opposing party shall immediately provide the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: JUN ZHI LI VS. RESIDENT MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 23, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT WESTERN MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY, WESTERN MUTUAL INSURANCE GROUP, RESIDENCE MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrers to the reformation cause of action and bad faith based on the reformed contract cause of action are overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads facts regarding the terms of the intended contract. Mercury Ins. Co. v. Pearson (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 1064, 1073, is distinguishable because in that action, the reformation claim was based on a single conclusory allegation. The demurer to the fraud and negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to plead those claims with more particularity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
